zh.siteSubtitle: 意大利西多会修道院长与神秘主义者（约1135-1202）
zh.homeTitle: 菲奥雷的约阿基姆是谁？
zh.homeIntro1: 菲奥雷的约阿基姆（Joachim of Fiore，约1135年—1202年3月30日），意大利卡拉布里亚地区的西多会修道院长、神学家与神秘主义者。他在卡拉布里亚山区创立了菲奥雷的圣乔瓦尼修道院，并在那里潜心著述。
zh.homeIntro2: 约阿基姆最具影响力的思想是他的「三纪神学」——将人类历史分为三个递进时代：圣父之纪（旧约时代，律法统治）、圣子之纪（新约时代，教会统治）、以及即将降临的圣灵之纪（修道士的冥想时代，自由与爱的统治）。他相信历史是一个不断向更高灵性境界演进的过程。
zh.homeIntro3: 约阿基姆的思想深刻影响了方济各灵修派、千禧年主义运动以及西方历史哲学传统。黑格尔的辩证法、马克思的历史唯物主义、甚至纳粹德国的「第三帝国」概念，都可追溯到他的三纪框架。第四次拉特兰公会议（1215年）谴责了他的三位一体学说，但他的历史预言思想持续影响至今。
zh.coreQuote: 「圣父之纪属于奴仆，圣子之纪属于儿子，圣灵之纪属于朋友。第一纪是知识的时代，第二纪是智慧的时代，第三纪将是圆满理解的时代。」
keyPointsList: 约1135年 —— 生于卡拉布里亚的切利科，后成为西多会修道士
1183-1184年 —— 在科拉措的卡萨马里修道院获得三次灵性顿悟，形成三纪神学核心
约1190年 —— 完成三部核心著作：《新旧约协调论》《启示录注释》《十弦琴》
1191年 —— 创立菲奥雷的圣乔瓦尼修道院
1202年 —— 逝世于卡拉布里亚，身后著作广泛流传
1215年 —— 第四次拉特兰公会议谴责其三位一体学说
1254年 —— 方济各灵修派热拉尔多将约阿基姆思想发扬为「永恒福音」
sourceList: 预言内容整理自约阿基姆三部核心著作：《新旧约协调论》(Concordia Novi ac Veteris Testamenti)、《启示录注释》(Expositio in Apocalypsim)、《十弦琴》(Psalterium Decem Chordarum)（Wikipedia）
学术参考：Marjorie Reeves, Joachim of Fiore and the Prophetic Future (1976)（Internet Archive）
验证评价基于历史研究文献和教会史记录
编者观点不代表学术定论
站点图标采用三个互锁圆环——约阿基姆最著名的三位一体图解，象征圣父、圣子、圣灵三个位格的相互关联与统一

en.homeIntro1: Joachim of Fiore (c.1135 – March 30, 1202) was an Italian Cistercian abbot, theologian, and mystic from Calabria. He founded the monastery of San Giovanni in Fiore in the Calabrian mountains, where he devoted himself to writing.
en.homeIntro2: Joachim's most influential idea was his 'theology of the three ages' — dividing human history into three progressive eras: the Age of the Father (Old Testament, rule of law), the Age of the Son (New Testament, rule of the Church), and the coming Age of the Spirit (age of monastic contemplation, rule of freedom and love). He believed history was a process of continuous ascent toward higher spiritual understanding.
en.homeIntro3: Joachim's thought profoundly influenced the Franciscan Spirituals, millenarian movements, and the Western philosophy of history. Hegel's dialectic, Marx's historical materialism, and even the Nazi concept of the 'Third Reich' can be traced back to his three-age framework. The Fourth Lateran Council (1215) condemned his doctrine of the Trinity, but his prophetic philosophy of history continues to resonate today.
en.coreQuote: "The first age was the age of knowledge, the second the age of wisdom, and the third will be the age of full understanding. The first was servile obedience, the second filial obedience, the third is freedom."
keyPointsList: c.1135 —— Born in Celico, Calabria; later became a Cistercian monk
1183-1184 —— Three spiritual illuminations at Casamari Abbey, forming the core of his three-age theology
c.1190 —— Completed three major works: Concordia, Expositio in Apocalypsim, Psalterium Decem Chordarum
1191 —— Founded the monastery of San Giovanni in Fiore
1202 —— Died in Calabria; posthumous works widely circulated
1215 —— Fourth Lateran Council condemned his Trinitarian doctrine
1254 —— Franciscan Spiritual Gerard of Borgo San Donnino promoted Joachim's ideas as the 'Eternal Gospel'
sourceList: Prophecies compiled from Joachim's three major works: Concordia Novi ac Veteris Testamenti, Expositio in Apocalypsim, Psalterium Decem Chordarum (Wikipedia)
Academic reference: Marjorie Reeves, Joachim of Fiore and the Prophetic Future (1976) (Internet Archive)
Verification based on historical research and church history records
Editorial opinions do not represent academic consensus
Site icon: three interlocking circles — Joachim's famous Trinitarian diagram symbolizing the interconnection and unity of Father, Son, and Holy Spirit
